textByHash.015d7b3fcaec7fc7ecbc73d8d6e67014928120d8: I will tell you, , but not just yet. Finish showing me around Lumbridge and then I'll tell you.
textByHash.0448ac389627870c313c8dfe2e9abcfd8f885a64: Let's have another look around the main area. Maybe there's something we missed.
textByHash.04b332f46921cdc6beb4228f236579f2d7ecb2bd: What was it Juna said to me? 'Your destiny is to lead all goblins into a new age.'
textByHash.086fa22dd17e60b67d40c14fd876178f13ec3463: Let's have a look at what this box is, then let's go home. There's nothing here for anyone any more.
textByHash.0a9fa35962ca09cc32d83a8888c82843b0467018: It's really amazing to think that these things were used by my ancestors hundreds of years ago. Tegdak thinks that some of them may even have been brought down here from the surface by the early Dorgeshuun!
textByHash.0d01c0fc77a718d58ac56edf00d51b9ccd462704: What are we going down there again for? We know where we need to go -- the Lumbridge water mill!
textByHash.0db8a160210d1a313f0d9d0b9ba9a708c5d9be53: The guards caught you but I managed to slip away. Here, I'll let you out!
textByHash.0dd6bf27b94c3793360cafefed358caf03e203c5: Well that confirms it then. This fungus ring might be just what we need!
textByHash.0ed8f86813cbf50195c87b68b9136050cfe42ade: I'm sorry! I didn't realize. I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun!
textByHash.100e8eb080b949eb598094c96a4cf048b3077ad9: You'll have to find a new one from somewhere then.
textByHash.10f7df2b86d78c1b0c78e29259a2ff967467f46f: I had a moving-over-distance sphere with me, but they confiscated it when they put me in here. They didn't even know what it was. They were just frightened that it might explode.
textByHash.11152477fb488bee473acb575ede8d5c420ed872: I've hardly SEEN the surface yet! just dragged me back down here!
textByHash.13f61fce7c764adcd041cc83851a0db590054f69: Maybe when I return I can convince them to open the gates of the city.
textByHash.1402548f325d64e77af428346a7203eecc6631d5: Oh, I know what it is! It's an axe head!
textByHash.14250eb9e5d21dde884aaf02ddd832194be9a872: And not all surface-dwellers are bad. Without the city would have been destroyed for sure!
textByHash.14683d7d26baa7ade46d2addcb0ee6370f6ea316: I've been helping Oldak with his experiments!
textByHash.151cb43e5cd81329f95cc064c42d7159ac8db077: This is so exciting! Perhaps later I'll come back and sample lots of different surface foods!
textByHash.158817c6122e651ce85057561e642cdc3b948b67: Why more so than shooting them in the front?
textByHash.15a91a099f58126a31176757e82fbb4838c51e9d: Anyway, you should help Oldak out with the machine. He wants me to stand here so that when the portal opens, it'll have a goblin right next to it.
textByHash.18b8714e2615c2d60834759878527ea23a6cf128: Stop talking about me like I'm not here!
textByHash.1a18b8f5e76c70da319dfee22fdbc665bf8ab0fe: It was... it wasn't like anything.
textByHash.1b49e2cf67e06098281be26e98f6aecd764dc408: We did it! My city is safe! Now we can follow that tunnel south back to the mines!
textByHash.1c5ec780699cd01859eea244908cb207269720e7: Aren't you going to put on your HAM disguise? You might be able to do without one but I don't want you giving me away!
textByHash.1dc5480dcc793e214d89a208451a8a47afbbb119: How are your studies going, Oldak?
textByHash.1e1684cc793e94ed953e3040fdf7746bf0180c30: ...And when they opened up the giant frog's stomach they found all the missing silver, and Mistag's brooch!
textByHash.1e2b4290c28dea37acdb8c887c3cc927e5675fee: Now let's get back to Dorgesh-Kaan to tell them what happened! We can go south along the tunnel Sigmund has dug.
textByHash.1e5f8e50e080377d6125dfad1a2ff278f6e4f228: That's right! We've been cut off from the outside world for thousands of years but now we're back!
textByHash.1f4c2a0738c5d9cde0dd84d1dac07b6067914c89: You're back! Any luck at the temple? Have you found out where Yu'biusk is?
textByHash.20aa90f1822fb19c4b6eab0602dad23924585ad7: I can't believe this! The goblin homeland! This could be somewhere safe for goblins like Grubfoot. A new home, free of war!
textByHash.21b2bf3e6d8b3e6efafbad48ffa5516c58feddc5: You've got to get them to turn their backs on me so I can shoot them without them seeing me draw my crossbow.
textByHash.2296ff3464a2ac07c3c189a75548921b0c447185: It's a surface shop full of exotic surface goods! What are you selling?
textByHash.22aa8023a05a16750eb9a0e36733d0f341beade2: I don't know. But there must be something around here we can use...
textByHash.231cf0854a08a6b69780fb0433906df30946c5e3: We haven't finished there actually. Do you think you could teleport us there again?
textByHash.2406e46c1ab972d8faa3ce6c1f2cc0c9f467be14: And they're all lapping it up! No one challenges him or even asks questions. They're just sitting there while he fills their minds with hate!
textByHash.2633339c67b947669f253ffc3038e1fbfe8920e1: Axes are these things they use on the surface for cutting down trees. I think they can be used as weapons too.
textByHash.283fe4bcaf971bff15946905edeebc1de10f98fd: It got away in the end. But it was the biggest I'd ever seen!
textByHash.28622a8ab5c3ed994ea12ba7e60521ca3b74fb08: I talked to Juna about it, but she wouldn't tell me anything. She just said, 'You will know, when the time is right.'
textByHash.2973cac8e1b2d88a26067404603783b770fbecaf: I've been thinking about my destiny...
textByHash.2a0c8e26264e5d15231034ed190773d7c2f1170a: Could this be it? Could this be my destiny? Could this be why I was marked by the Tears of Guthix?
textByHash.2a3cd387420c256febe5ca95d1551eb4dd05c577: I'll wait for you back in the castle then.
textByHash.2adca2c41559c7b9c9b6d980a652b6490b2b694a: No! Listen! The god commanded the Dorgeshuun to fight a battle they could not win!
textByHash.2c54a62cbbccba88eba99bd9ca6e98d7d8445a69: I don't think that guard's going to move away from the door. I think we need to approach him from different directions...
textByHash.2ce4219ff462ec112d5f3167af5cc18fee6fbb6f: A helmet? So those are eye-holes? They look too small.
textByHash.2d21ee8765fecd802052b457136f5c96b69cce4c: 'All goblins.' Not just the Dorgeshuun, all goblins...
textByHash.2db978af6e4620c68d4e8eedfcde9254cbf187e6: Goblins... goblins of the Goblin Village. I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun. You may know the legend of the Dorgeshuun, but let me tell you our own version.
textByHash.2dfcf7e6a09ce74a2d22b0491a8d2fd03b9c557c: So where is this promised land, Grubfoot?
textByHash.2e28c992d4edb3cc6bfc9c89e9476e5f36b94f80: The important thing is we stopped him! Now let's smash that machine and then get back to Dorgesh-Kaan!
textByHash.2ee97a529f1bcb8a91c77a481f58ed82ada57c9f: Come on , let's get back to the Goblin Village on the surface! Shall we go?
textByHash.30196bac78a685e3a526731a75597e6e66603d28: There must be something around here. It can't all be dead... can it?
textByHash.324a2d9a55624a8da00cbcd7d479035cf788b169: I'll meet you back in Dorgesh-Kaan.
textByHash.34c82d899a801187e1786c4ae31c915ae97ccc51: We'll need two sets of full robes: shirt, robe, hood, cloak, gloves, boots and badge.
textByHash.353637a1c4fb726b826b77a94ff34f8c2bd105e0: Perhaps I should take you to see it?
textByHash.358ee327511dc9741986b2c4f4b39e3d86ec5d04: They tell me I'm an expert with the bone crossbow!
textByHash.35adc7da7c89aa04b242ba9899d9fadfaca5a0ec: Let's go! We've got to get to the Lumbridge water mill so we can foil Sigmund's plan!
textByHash.399cdd10737472a82d4b74bb0875bd05557a6160: We don't have any of those things in Dorgesh-Kaan! They sound so interesting!
textByHash.39afc45824b1f97f34437e803003ae60703bbc33: And I've always wondered... what's beyond the next cave? What's at the end of every tunnel? The surface is a whole new world to explore, and I can't wait!
textByHash.39fcc8f20da169191950b1c203dc17153cfcb544: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Pleased to meet you!
textByHash.3a1f35ae67cb0e4667aa68a77765ace81120eff7: I don't know what those things are! Are they exotic surface foods?
textByHash.3a49204fc1096593424b7e574d82e510639869f1: We've not got time to talk now, Kazgar! , you've got to take me to the Lumbridge water mill at once!
textByHash.3a6f8f285948ed8a58c4ed4a364d048f2f3331f8: Those must be the parts for the machine they're going to use to flood my city!
textByHash.3b73b8442e1fd03b0f6458b0debde3ad3b06c1f5: I don't know! I've never visited the surface before!
textByHash.3d666eb45fd4ff802f28017dcfb46b0afa4a81f0: I'm not much good at thieving either, I'm afraid. You'd better get some practice.
textByHash.3e41a41a2be1a8b449bb6d952bfde4f1505edc21: Well... a bit. All our legends say it's a terrible place.
textByHash.3e755118425e3edc6643b85c5a0ca9f50b6f0b2c: I've been training on giant frogs!
textByHash.3e78509915dfc5f24c3d95499eb32bb77eabc8a2: It's a special technique with the bone crossbow. If we both get out of this alive I'll teach you!
textByHash.4082cf595f87fc95da91d22dc850d01aaea9d63b: We think it's a magical weapon from the surface. Can you decipher the symbols on it?
textByHash.43da58b310746602420e7f5a52e5be9982fe2e4a: So far on the surface we haven't seen any... you know... gods.
textByHash.440307527d9bf76b2f29d14c92f1b178feb46bf5: That's the last of them! Now to listen to what's happening in the meeting room!
textByHash.4495d64073f501116584bb3fd1848ab37718dc5c: Why don't you take me to a shop? I promised Tignik I would bring back a souvenir from the surface.
textByHash.4537eb93389c5e91858e87e5ba746998faa9324c: This must lead to the Dorgeshuun mines! Sigmund must mean the water to run through here and flood the city!
textByHash.461e2b639acc9918b9f6d1dc5e71b9b3daa2b4e6: Actually, I'm a little nervous about what I'll find there...
textByHash.471495825b450392ffe175735534c34a214626c0: But there's no time for that now. Let's get to the water mill!
textByHash.4a7efc051d229e684aa56020b29987b07fedfa05: Yes. I thought all surface goblins thought war was good.
textByHash.4ad9c06ffb635dada257083a75dc920d75b47d9b: Then maybe you could lead one of them past me...
textByHash.4b145cea36a30f28d67775dcac786dcdc2fe0f32: Hey. This is incredible! I can't believe we're really going to go to Yu'biusk, the Land of the Goblins!
textByHash.4b6c64136fcb84f5858831f324f0994d167cc70f: Don't leave me! I can't defeat Sigmund without you!
textByHash.4b86f45b34bc374cd19de42d91a00f9385250d06: Perhaps some other time. Right now I'm just looking around.
textByHash.4c7080ea1d18a0534e4bd4dd55375e2c185bf78b: All right. I'll go back and help finish the dig.
textByHash.4cc0d28c4d3522194e6d5dde8bdd9112d491c90b: We Dorgeshuun don't believe in an afterlife. At least, if there is one we don't claim to know about it. And if anything happened to me while I was dead then I don't remember it.
textByHash.4db246a61b00ea7a893a58f61d76ad7b6097b6fa: is a friend. You can tell him.
textByHash.4e8b884e06dcf90f284576240450815b3329047c: Look at the design! That's a lot like the design of the goblin armour I saw on the surface.
textByHash.4ec1edd51d7598e23b0b73c5a09dbc509402d2e0: Pleased to meet you! I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun!
textByHash.5168068dd7edc0ffbdf251cf73fbc9ed9e6bb295: There's no need to go that way! We can go south along the tunnel to the Dorgeshuun mine.
textByHash.51db6a02941bb56318c9e2a3df0796f87a782862: I could hide in one of those crates, and then you could carry me downstairs as if you were carrying some of the machinery.
textByHash.5207d299c261e7b1d3bfe89a54b5f74e5c57a6fa: I promise, Grubfoot. I won't tell anyone where the temple is.
textByHash.54f17bfc95db96648eb5fb5fbeded693fe76bf6e: This is brilliant! I'm sure you won't regret this!
textByHash.55c3e1880d9c1b9994ace2db4f05143566768767: Yes! The Dorgeshuun tribe? We live underground. We've been cut off from other goblins for generations but now we're back!
textByHash.57ab9c5b9cde028c96ea14ae762790a6f8dda231: This is really it, isn't it? Grubfoot's dream was just that, a dream. All Yu'biusk is now is an empty wasteland.
textByHash.59c351282f3a7e6a37fd4b231fa073cbdb1481a4: I don't have an account! All my belongings are safe at home in Dorgesh-Kaan!
textByHash.5a058eabc2b59b38f00169da94edbb05642b727a: Someday I will, but I don't think the council will agree to open the doors any time soon.
textByHash.5cdddffc898353e88bc0de3a083925f1c949a224: Never mind. I don't think we need to go to Al Kharid anyway!
textByHash.5d4ccdfb3bd8f4e202f326d6152250c6923ba41c: Get that guard to turn his back on me. Maybe if you walk around him he'll keep facing you.
textByHash.5e7ada4799e2c0916a4c7991047f4a0a147119ab: The surface is a strange and wonderful place! We should embrace it, not shut ourselves off!
textByHash.5eb096cd46ecab1f85d1f8e604b47776b89a3f7a: No... I'm sure the threat will be dealt with soon.
textByHash.5ec8316465cc3323f3574edaa81471b610eadc83: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! What is this place?
textByHash.5f666ea55ef820c26ce5b88804a82220236ae9ab: Oh well. Let me know when you're ready.
textByHash.5feab4e020765fd3b81a2ba0e38a4822e05ecaf6: But I'm not sure I'd want to live under it for too long. It's kind of scary!
textByHash.61dd1212bca319fbaf078483a671a2cd42029909: Oh. A holy mace of the War God.
textByHash.63068073584452f81166249e6170cb945b6c90e2: I went on another visit to Lumbridge! I still can't convince other Dorgeshuun to go to the surface with me, though.
textByHash.6332d092d33a6cca066579fe82c2b975c565398b: You said you'd show me around Lumbridge before we went down there, Azacor.
textByHash.636b32858d281a182f1d55e21089c0d045e13fb3: The Big High War God happened.
textByHash.641e620c1aa57579498089a770f5504dfc018ee7: Don't they? I have no idea what to expect!
textByHash.649ffc5117e0082179ab767bbf397375aeca34a6: Hi ! Have you got our disguises yet?
textByHash.662ee1f4538a67b88003b8acea8167c94bca0fa5: When I picked it up I felt strange... it was as if someone was watching to me, urging me to do something, but I don't know what!
textByHash.693319643c57e327e2e30f5b27db4c750a51e051: No thank you, I have a lantern of my own!
textByHash.6960ff457c1e4b194cebc2ec5bbfe538e347037c: What are we waiting for? Let's get to the HAM hideout and find out what they're planning!
textByHash.699b02b5704e0670fbdbec98eaf581cbd8e0936a: And now my life's a mystery too. What was that destiny Juna talked about?
textByHash.69f5d03e2e6d6ce75d8dbccf9458974c5cd7bd8e: Look at that stress fracture. It looks like this shield broke pretty easily.
textByHash.6a0a9990bc256bbd86692059e4a4eab8995b7431: This 'wood' is an odd material. It's heavier than bone but it doesn't seem to be as brittle. It would be good for making crossbows out of!
textByHash.6bd71f61636dcada282629808cfbf08c4109cb0b: Over there. There's a trapdoor hidden by dirt.
textByHash.6f584f4074c2cab3574ac0a8633b06245c00aac9: Not yet. There must be another area where the HAM people make their plans. We should try to find it.
textByHash.6f9f9a078bfb425bc12daa80f9aba8c719d73239: I gave it to Tegdak. If you want to have it again you should talk to him.
textByHash.723a91a01a20d0aebfc6c1c3fe2313369a3e6570: I wish I could show it to you!
textByHash.7253ddc87eaae6d9f85308e5ae7cb5e6130f25cc: I don't know. All we have to go on is Grubfoot's dream, which isn't much. Still, not long now until we find out!
textByHash.72ca7a4ec1d141b1f52a73cea5281d4bd79493ba: What are we waiting for? Let's go!
textByHash.732ec051660a60ca8d49da8366b7a9709c3dc72d: All right, let's go! I can't wait to see all around Lumbridge!
textByHash.7443abdc34aa07af27f838bc768fe11ed6810754: I've seen enough of Lumbridge now. Let's get to the HAM lair and see if they're up to anything!
textByHash.7510c8f70262164193f6c104a9bef814069eddea: I think I've seen enough of Lumbridge now. If you like I'll tell you about the mark on my forehead.
textByHash.754eb7110f0ab7343ffa23b7e81f4d56962602f3: But that's a bad reason not to visit it. Maybe the legends are wrong. Or even if they're right, we should know for sure.
textByHash.76bbcd3a098525dc7786cb852c1d6dbcdb8602dd: It must be my imagination, but I can almost feel the power coming off it, like it's calling out to me.
textByHash.794607cb6033a622376985e804cab8e1f0dae9fe: Listen to what that speaker's saying!
textByHash.79bf61f6c907b049e24ea995e31343f141bc0fd5: I thought this might be it. I really did. I thought this place might finally give me some answers, but it's just another dead end.
textByHash.7b211b878c99a212859de9a659772d3cccf42f9f: The room where the HAM members make their plans must be down here somewhere. We've got to find it without getting caught!
textByHash.7bf0a9a598ad5d0bb13acbe89ee3b153eec5afc7: We can't go into the HAM lair yet! I'm not wearing my disguise!
textByHash.7c2c8876bdab8bf61f2cc772a3b83f9c091f7bf4: We were never going to attack you! YOU nearly attacked US!
textByHash.7c8661e73c73f5b78ac32970d6e8bd7b99c514f6: You might find one of those balls useful if you wanted to visit me! Perhaps you should talk to Oldak.
textByHash.7e4e60ab8427e363e95aa60dcac42a1f6de29622: They didn't just k-kill me quickly. They were enjoying themselves. The light was so bright I couldn't see but I could hear them laughing...
textByHash.7f8388b7207ad4892e0b3fe6d81a0a808037b78d: I promised Tignik I would bring a souvenir back from the surface. Will you take me to a shop?
textByHash.7ff48b288753bd97867b8efe9d1d2a1ffd303a94: Let's get them! I need to save my city and I want to get my revenge!
textByHash.7ffefd14d31cb79d5ec821441a96c2d5a3ad5167: Shall we go and talk to the scribe about this ancient mace now?
textByHash.8022ce42025fdb177944f1efce26f1680162c97a: I don't know what wool is, but I'm sure it's fascinating!
textByHash.8041850f1120ca4e9ebf3384557ce995e1da87aa: I don't want to train prayer! I can manage without asking the gods for favours!
textByHash.8080e485159c9f0c716624b0b7c208efc0536388: I'm having a look at the things that are being dug up here!
textByHash.812183768f40179a46e972fe6b5a3a552d2d1fb1: Perhaps we could talk to the Duke of Lumbridge. Headman Ur-Tag asked me to give him his regards.
textByHash.813cecc328ff211c7515a9f5549f3fe21ab07adf: We're getting a bit far away from Lumbridge. I'd like to see the rest of the world someday but not right now. Let's stick to Lumbridge for now.
textByHash.821e06df5fce57e900ae6a08fb5a986c3c61959b: I visited the goblin village again. I wanted to learn more about their legend of the 'Chosen Commander'.
textByHash.82dad1aa37b0acfdf53878a67c19aaef5934ff5f: Sure thing! Come on, , let's go talk to the scribe
textByHash.836914ae3a89d3f108bc2d06e52230ad4028ef3e: Why are we going away from the mill? We should get down there and save my city!
textByHash.857186a9f904e788267e774a3529e798f11e84fb: But I didn't find out any more. They couldn't decide whether I really was the Chosen Commander or not, and they just got into a big argument about it.
textByHash.85b81f58623dab05a67496143e3c3017884ab7ec: We can get through this if we work together. But I can't get too close to the guards or they'll see I'm not human.
textByHash.8623e90c8984f566c7b256c419c7849974ca1ce0: Your art! Your philosophy! Your way of life! Now that the godwars are over you must have been able to build a peaceful civilisation!
textByHash.89860dad456144eb3211a88e2afcc85d537032b4: The city is safe! The HAM group was plotting against us, but we stopped them!
textByHash.8a61f3c189dd1ab8f03a5b009159240c63491be5: Well... our legends all talk about the gods living on the surface, but since I've been up here I haven't see any. Is there anyone around here who might know about them?
textByHash.8a65bd3e3e859e1c8dea6d17b48eb29fae768b80: You should pick up your pet thingy. It would only get in the way.
textByHash.8ac0a8c2fa18cc143561ce05c7a5075aa7f74153: I managed to upset the high priest. He said he'd tell me about Yu'biusk if I passed a test on goblin religion. I don't think he liked my answers.
textByHash.8acb6a7aeef2d4a0dc6b173289d9dc3de36ca3b6: Sorry about that! Oldak's calculations aren't always exact!
textByHash.8d316a0914ead2152c5f7f7c53ac5ca0bf61c838: The entrance to the secret temple must be around here somewhere.
textByHash.8d596afb377419e30affcc9c139aa02de5d8b5e5: I'm so looking forward to seeing the surface. But the Council insists that I get the disguise first.
textByHash.8e56bcf05466f4bab24466f4190c23754ab071d6: Thank goodness you're all right! Now let's finish off Sigmund and his cronies before they flood the city!
textByHash.8e97d4222bb8422edee24b35912b8bf8f326333f: It's a pity some people up here don't like me. I suppose I should have expected that.
textByHash.905df8cd12e1866c412baca5553b03e93ee22e2a: I'm sorry. I'd like to show you around the city, but the council has decided not to let any surface-dwellers in yet.
textByHash.9178cc6be6feceb969d779a2ad60546943bb31f7: I hope I get ranging XP!
textByHash.91d4dc34e13e84ef4352da4077d607d39b764bec: Do it quickly! We've got to stop Sigmund from destroying my city!
textByHash.929c6200d409a39af0a27503a1c90aba2822dd02: If you can get to the other side of that guard you can make him turn around so I can shoot him. Make sure another guard doesn't catch you though!
textByHash.92e57876ebdfb01a0a405adce3c9438b1cd3178a: I'm not sure a Dorgeshuun would want to marry a human!
textByHash.93449d3fbcd32f9406b4ebef9322ab998da176fc: Not right now, Oldak. I'm helping !
textByHash.9371f8ec371d0b6b773350dfaa0fb0376bfffe33: So this is the HAM hideout. It's so squalid and dirty! I don't know how they can bear to live here.
textByHash.944a3f3474c082a3497140ef913895b5e3235a4e: We, er, we're your distant cousins? We want to learn about your culture.
textByHash.958c400ce2433640bd40323169e65637a8f1f138: Not right now, Oldak. We wanted to see if you could make Moving-Over-Distance Spheres to somewhere other than the city.
textByHash.9811c0815df0807a36118de523ba43d0778e524a: Where did you go? We should follow the tunnel south to Dorgesh-Kaan!
textByHash.9848b76a3155c4f32d3ce24a0a675fb718cad376: I can't believe we're going to the Goblin Village on the surface! I'm finally going to see how my distant relatives live!
textByHash.9901da107cf12ba008f6d4d49c25986294c50ad2: Quick! Untie me before the train comes!
textByHash.991a7b6ddcec53efba51873b4322d751247b435c: says that's a kind of weapon.
textByHash.9af19cf41430d055d27e6f14dbe1598ac8e38501: Oh yes. My whole city is overrun by goblins.
textByHash.9b5bab139c54fae4ea583ce5a1051e9543672b85: I've got it! I'll hold onto it until we've shown it to the scribe. Shall we go?
textByHash.9c008e2b00a90772adf0cd6aee974b5f402a23cd: If they're the basic ingredients, why don't you already have them?
textByHash.9c1f30a4d85e2f7952619ea0ee42729f8f121263: We can't just leave that machine there for someone else to complete! We should smash it beyond repair before returning home!
textByHash.9c88b59d704b24e29c6455b6be089eaae704ba6f: And then suddenly I was down in the cave and you were here. There was nothing in between.
textByHash.9c9bfc27e862e2f226966d489be3fdc7644c5a4b: What are you doing? Noo! Don't kill me! , help me!
textByHash.9d3d24f8b6cfd8e0b0a14f54805d03858c0ce85f: Well, I know iron and silver. What other ores are there?
textByHash.9da4cfaad55972d44fd49f8a17ae63fb1f107a2d: We don't like bright lights. They hurt our eyes.
textByHash.9e9d798e5deb6e724168f5add88e64359b7e5999: I'll take care of him! Just tell me when his back is to us...
textByHash.9f9415d527a9de5302febe08360b6639287f03cb: I'm Zanik of the Dorgeshuun! Headman Ur-Tag sends his greetings.
textByHash.a066b3af50a4b90c79efbfdd9abf9c899e65f438: A wood bucket! And a wood hammer! And a newcomer map! I brought some surface money.
textByHash.a09c9a6f384074bb47fefc1a867b283e68217836: Shall we go see Oldak now? I'm sure he'll have a way of getting us to the Goblin Village quickly!
textByHash.a0f1c43ab46e8eb48a39d923cbb0a516cb831189: We must be missing something. This can't be it...
textByHash.a13ce5a79e0105b79b7ebabdbdc2ad2c44b2c88d: Are you thinking what I'm thinking?
textByHash.a1a6c50eb5851924f4833c66584abdd1206ae976: I can't wait to see the surface! But first you need to get us both sets of HAM robes.
textByHash.a230f765cacef2bf7ce53f5d59904da741cb4e24: You ought to free both your hands so you can carry the crate. I'm afraid I might be heavy!
textByHash.a265426fe7f1d4b3411ee3124a26d178412d2abe: The guards confiscated it when they put me in here. It must be somewhere nearby.
textByHash.a29b24e579a38f9a9f60d1475ae6c91d7410f944: Don't give up! I know you'll work it out.
textByHash.a2b83b8af07f883897cbe7854af6cd6291a2d1cb: I will tell you, Azacor, but not just yet. Finish showing me around Lumbridge and then I'll tell you.
textByHash.a2cf81a8c1d2cec90043afe5c6831af17d131184: I'd rather not travel all that way over the surface, though. I wonder if Oldak could use magic to help us get there?
textByHash.a62d5cf977120ff0cc850bc9deb9f37e49200b6b: The shots came from the top of that tower! Can you see anything?
textByHash.a6b5596f7ddc22a5bf38de82bab4492c61de838d: I'll show you the way through to the city. Wait till we tell Mistag about what happened!
textByHash.a6fe7b45c00e840a7f9cb841184d416a12ad5f8f: Yes! We're another tribe of goblins who live underground. We've been cut off from the surface for generations but now we're back.
textByHash.a771d0bbf6fd247a62956df97ddd8514a0e8ea87: I think I can hear something happening behind the double doors. We should listen.
textByHash.a7934d76fd2b012c2803736c072f8c2ac5278eac: What are we doing down here? I want to see the surface!
textByHash.a9d9ddcaaa341cd2b0c29291477542045d56f6e1: Get him to face away from me...
textByHash.aa4f46ff365ab90fd3bb861149e1b9bacbd86ba2: To be honest I don't know. It happened when...
textByHash.acd9f029e7df0f17ebf52c440cab3d91420167ae: I've got a strange feeling about this mace. Maybe when we find out about it, it will shed some light onto my destiny. Come on, let's show it to the scribe.
textByHash.ae94b98ffe9f5475cfbe688bafc260a5b20b3b76: I should reunite the two sides of the race!
textByHash.afeedbe56bb825d1a84ad4218022e3be92625bee: What's a church? And what is Holy Saradomin?
textByHash.affd49b4124ce9e3343b861a362cbd32dbe2b4b8: I ought to visit the Duke of Lumbridge while I'm here. Ur-Tag asked me to give him his regards.
textByHash.b0c8113f135386786796c4dd00e62acd61a8b12e: Come find me in the city if you ever want to chat. I'm going to go and think about what Juna said about my destiny. I don't know what it will involve, but I have a feeling we will adventure together again!
textByHash.b121d80bda091d0f5f95b5de5c7043d2f86ef0e2: helped us to finish the dig! Look what she dug up!
textByHash.b1447d539cd4a005ce3bf2859f052f954a1aee6e: Maybe someday we'll find out! Oh, hello!
textByHash.b3b7cd431fd47122b0cf57ec719c1fd489f797c5: I can't get anywhere without coming into their line of fire, but I think you could get around the side of the buildings to the ladder.
textByHash.b3e88fca5c3d71a6c466a92e4c7bf2899a5f435c: That guard in the east corridor... if I wait at the end of the corridor then you could lead him past me...
textByHash.b5d8c545b340174cb4443ba29faabae27207a6fc: Maybe that's true. I should try to approach things with an open mind!
textByHash.b7d326aa2dfe90ab7e3abb3ae84a1b42b94920fe: You must introduce me to some of the surface people!
textByHash.b7e4173070d856aea70c30d274c1e7439c1358a7: It's so bright! I can't look at it!
textByHash.b8ef10b54d82504cd3e9ce97293ac62ad1daf9fb: You've still got that ancient mace...
textByHash.b9133981b7914895d2969139e2b31b832cc09d73: It's so enclosed, it could almost be underground. Let's go outside! I want to see the sun!
textByHash.b93719f4390a05d8481a5655d4550d6eabffce63: I won't let you destroy my home, Sigmund!
textByHash.b967940007286e58f79a20e9ad53caecb74a6c9e: It's amazing! The sky is so huge!
textByHash.b99989460ad92866c98366dc2d15919eac2cc99a: Okay. I'll meet you back at the cave entrance.
textByHash.bb400b4edb218e1e37678c840013a68196315222: But it's amazing to think that there's nothing above me. Just the sun and the air and empty space, forever...
textByHash.bd6fc5d313044b8862f26b9088da361db996a5f6: All right. I'll wait for you in the castle cellar.
textByHash.bde480404f9bb529f830406a4a4e7a3484b18d13: No, there was no cavern ceiling. Just a great expanse of blue sky!
textByHash.be1e814241e816092511d70002127e5d53fa1947: What are we doing here? We should get to the Goblin Village!
textByHash.bed78aacf609b583aceb9ecb68a975f01703ec79: I'll say. You can tell me all about it later. For now, I could do with a bit of a hand getting out of here.
textByHash.c10c3a74cd97b09e86ad37daa2ffc82bada08ac6: A giant frog found its way into the mines, but I helped the guards to drive it off!
textByHash.c115bf0e4ac491755c50097f4c15b1b272d9055d: What's that thing with the wheel?
textByHash.c1552357241a2856b78eb803da953442729eebe3: Are you ready to finish our tour now?
textByHash.c1ee6821363986f701e1248ca308012f3f60beb3: All right. But not here. Let's go back into Lumbridge town.
textByHash.c2877b52d1e767d566c4102dad31ec0520864717: One moment Sigmund and his thugs were... you know...
textByHash.c2dba6e5288d4a8824712d2fafad2ce7eb6e6c7e: 'Sheep'! Finding all these strange surface creatures is so exciting!
textByHash.c2e57200b8a212c4f4f0e2a862df769c3ed07d16: Nevermind, player name. I don't think we need to go to Al Kharid anyway!
textByHash.c46bf8bef8bce503c3f8e67ed73678b605c994b0: Well I reckon we've got plenty of that.
textByHash.c67e649b6fee59e1f3f125ac354cd6bea72454de: Wow... The bucket and the hammer, what are they made of?
textByHash.c6d200f054f0d9ae179b4d827aeb5be51dac0fc1: Quick, , let's smash that drilling machine before Sigmund returns! Then we can return to Dorgesh- Kaan for a hero's welcome!
textByHash.c741cf29f847bfb124a62938354771da6d014de1: Actually I don't know anything about spells.
textByHash.c74c2d3e010167bdffb9289839f20b94d5898440: How should I know? You're the surface-dweller! Maybe you could steal them from the HAM people or something.
textByHash.c805557abeeb331adedd8ede0418632c5a198929: Where are we? I thought that ladder led to the surface.
textByHash.c81d35853a1e731c5746ddb896f098b9a93fd311: I don't need anything you've got to sell anyway!
textByHash.cb5a125abad64e0cf238fea6f3acfb504488b451: So this is the Goblin Village! It's so different from Dorgesh-Kaan, isn't it?
textByHash.ccc1474d114d2deba5567be78d828708a7ac17c9: All right. I'll wait for you back in Lumbridge castle cellar.
textByHash.cceb035f732ee0bbb5df4e2430b9e4dcc3c01cdc: So this is how goblins on the surface live! Come on, , lets talk to them!
textByHash.cdc7165279a8f42653a13dac875882d7ba529b94: Thank you for taking me on that adventure! I'll show you how to use the bone crossbow like I promised I would!
textByHash.cde3227620e4084def540a03d05cabbba5256b3b: I can manage without Saradomin, but thanks anyway.
textByHash.ce401ee261bf402450e0420455e4615904e9ef9a: That priest seemed friendly. I thought any servant of a god would be much more aggressive.
textByHash.cea403f26e282c8910478d72d8a67c3467dbe1ce: Duke Horacio seemed nice. But politics is just as boring up here as it is at home!
textByHash.d0161ddc64e6ab6170b0247b7d2f6a4ddbcf9574: Are there still goblins living on the surface, Player? Are there any near Lumbridge?
textByHash.d1d9b8175f8543ba169e81d5a0af460f036bbe8b: is a friend. You can tell her.
textByHash.d2481bc8adbf7acec7013cc2c140b8a56c31cb77: I don't think I could get through either. You'd better get some agility training done.
textByHash.d268200a34feb4d076e30d7b8a09c1601a38f6c9: No thank you! is showing me around!
textByHash.d2690ffd2746519457688a531a0ed023abd22a3c: Our history doesn't make a distinction. There was your own god who would kill you if you refused to fight, and someone else's god who would only kill you if you lost.
textByHash.d2e7a78e7359216c1a015a4fbe408b87c0e3222b: We've got to do something! They're going to--
textByHash.d4df903545b2655f0078e34cfc43254aa42ee4f1: Is the coast clear to get past?
textByHash.d5783859f0a7747bcb489d9651367b2d3101b036: Quick! Let's get to the water mill and stop Sigmund from destroying my home!
textByHash.d7c182785044cf887b737b5c72d793f942cd2114: For generations my people have lived with rock over our heads. But now I can see the sky!
textByHash.d7e6cf310358fbbd1dd00b5493c4c111d57ec492: Dorgeshuun history remembers them as real. But we don't know much detail. Our ancestors were just footsoldiers, they didn't know what they were fighting for.
textByHash.d8427657c154805edcf2234eb06f136077adb8fa: Our history does not remember his name. We know only that he was a violent god of war.
textByHash.d8aa4c0d5272609b6537fdf927f4bbe313d32065: If you don't mind, I'd rather not talk about it now. Maybe once you've shown me some of the surface.
textByHash.da6deea1de7eaecf3b9890a35d2502b22ed3a5dd: It looks a bit like a sword, doesn't it? I saw some people using them on the surface.
textByHash.da74efa4409f85c744013b8b0d58b573a9e7721c: Even so... if there's any chance there might be something in it, we have to investigate.
textByHash.dacdf715f561214b10fb990c70755420c86226a2: What kind of foods do you eat up here on the surface?
textByHash.db70914753e4723ed78191e77681a35e4602e9f9: Are there still goblins living on the surface? Are there any near Lumbridge?
textByHash.dbdf9f2d7b01a03967136389f22bfd81f80c6d0e: So what's your group planning to do about it?
textByHash.dbf0b576e549571c66577ee3b6181e901a20f808: You don't care about me at all!
textByHash.dd0da98a9c2e9f2cd88499d65b734970ea1e6442: Thousands of years ago, the Big High War God forced all goblins to fight in a terrible war.
textByHash.dd7f35f41e9cbc55f200debdda4c6ac55bb4a829: We're not too late! They're still assembling the machine. Now we've got to find some way to get down there and stop them.
textByHash.ddd62396dbbc246635ee402e666043303df4f3c0: I took his magic ball all over the caves and even to Lumbridge to test what happened when I broke it, and I always ended up back here.
textByHash.df36921b592801f32b794aebd3508e569640947b: I know! But the ruling council is so slow to make decisions. It's made up of old fogeys who are set in their ways.
textByHash.dfed2251b27052bdc07e73b847e15d34e6185fc1: Not yet. Come on! We should go and ask the goblin generals about it!
textByHash.e06574f94b0422bb9ac82db29be145bf27b8234d: I know! The fungus ring in the southern caves! Didn't you say that had magical properties, Oldak?
textByHash.e0c4062329a3dd5881e4f60556ddacc0ed56e465: It's probably nothing, but if there's even a small chance there's something in it, we have to investigate.
textByHash.e13b13b28b65c25a92dc64817eaafdf4c05a2126: Of course I do! I've mined lots of iron and silver in the Dorgeshuun mines!
textByHash.e13dd4df98d8933543e64abde3cc9574c6bb904a: Is that you? Do you have that moving-over-distance sphere?
textByHash.e24ca94323ecf8f731a4471bd913fe824cd5bddb: We would never do that! We don't tunnel upwards and we're very careful not to damage human cellars.
textByHash.e3f35a4a3dcc2eb5e2e37a766d0cf2cfc2cd53ef: Thousands of years ago the gods made our ancestors fight in horrible wars. It was only after we escaped from them that we were able to build a civilisation.
textByHash.e3fb769d759fd4bb5b13dd23b6d7668600817dd0: Why is peace for the weak?
textByHash.e41f9c86a4a658f9cf924c078b93cacb4947f830: Ever since then I've wondered what the sign meant. But perhaps you will help me to find out!
textByHash.e81392a01dc8a2a584422cfa8d1556595f2c040a: No, you don't understand. There was NO ceiling. The surface is right on top of the world! There's no more rock above it!
textByHash.e8de7db0f73dc0b62c43c7566c66e52de8fe65bd: We ought to talk to some of the HAM people. That's what we're here for, after all!
textByHash.e90c90e7d071a7fd8f8c4c9d87a53eedc3e99eb9: is a friend. You can tell them.
textByHash.e9a2282bf501e3c75619d311b10ed6e0a47b4881: We Dorgeshuun don't have any magical knowledge at all.
textByHash.ebb95a4d9504624753565b093f5f90c63ebf6467: The plot! , I've got to tell you what I heard outside the meeting room!
textByHash.ec0903977c88abeb4b3957a565cb67f9041c8d58: And what does it even mean, my destiny? Is it something I'm certain to do, or just something I ought to do? How can I know?
textByHash.ed0013492246b7d4c5aed29d669ea4b5f37f8cca: There are so many people around! You must introduce me to them!
textByHash.ed9cea2a93ca734b336c5f25f05d890e65e67c04: You don't need to think in those terms! We've lived for centuries without war, and without gods, and-
textByHash.edb251c708019c2f11c0ace0b51579f117cc3e59: Quick! Shoot him while I have him distracted!
textByHash.ef923612c0e46bd09e251dbc4c8412bdd94fef27: I thought you had the ancient goblin mace. But I found it in the caves.
textByHash.f0eb7d1698bc90a6c26310a20ef9396b1e57bd48: They're saying something about... they're planning something... something about a machine...
textByHash.f159483bbc2504a605fd70ec4738bfae599c3fea: Thank you! I'm sorry to leave you, but it looks like you'll have to find out about Yu'biusk on your own. Hopefully you have better luck with that high priest than I did.
textByHash.f389e7d670f1c75ef71b28a0e63c960843e04b4a: Then what am I doing here?
textByHash.f4fdff4c127bc163ba664bc2b843bad16a2b718b: Those poor goblins. It's hard to believe I'm the same species as them. No wonder the HAM people hate us if they assume we're like them.
textByHash.f6afea602f05cbe5fe7fa07adbb8b158a6fb3dcd: I recognize you. You were in Goblin Village, weren't you? You said you had a dream about me.
textByHash.f8950e7e5e80b8fa5f1e15a46f406b7f2e0b9495: Don't you dare go down there without me! Find some way to get us both down there so I can face Sigmund myself!
textByHash.fab2960ac63e356c7620c8067f11771a3bb8461e: Don't worry, I'm sure will help keep us safe from the H.A.M. people!
textByHash.fdd47cd1f208c6d1573487639dc6e302f3423ed8: Are you ready to go to the HAM cave now?
textByHash.fe0f6b6ee043ae2a7fbbe3f7a8b9fa79f281c662: There's no need for that yet. Why don't you show me around Lumbridge first? I'm so excited about visiting the surface!
textByHash.fe7b9098926e9982cf428ca97948cf43ca57ff00: There are lots of them! You know, the white fluffy things?
textByHash.ff61d50c7071f3c5e2a387b3526a4115fa2a50bd: Is that Sigmund? The one who tried to start the war between Lumbridge and the cave goblins?
